title: Running Attention in Parallel
concept: Multi-head attention runs multiple self-attention heads in parallel, letting the model focus on different aspects of context simultaneously.

exercises.prompt: When we say things run "in parallel," how do they happen?

options.text: At the exact same time (in parallel)
One by one in a long line
Only when the user asks it to

exercises.explain: Parallel processing means doing multiple tasks at the exact same time, which is how multi-head attention works.

cards.title: Multi-Tasking Eyes
cards.body: Imagine trying to read a book while only looking for action words, while also trying to remember who the characters are. Doing this one by one is slow. What if you had multiple eyes looking for different things all at once? That's the secret to how AI understands context so quickly!

cards.title: What is Multi-Head Attention?
cards.body: Multi-head attention splits the attention mechanism into multiple 'heads' that run in parallel. This allows the model to simultaneously look at different parts of a sentence, like matching a pronoun to its subject while also matching a verb to its object.

cards.title: The Movie Crew
cards.body: Think of multi-head attention like a movie crew analyzing a film scene. Instead of one director trying to watch everything, you have one person focusing on the actors' facial expressions, another watching the background details, and a third listening to the music. Because they work in parallel, they get a complete, rich understanding of the scene all at once!

cards.title: Parallel Perspectives
cards.body:
In the sentence: "The bank of the river had a bank building on it."
• Head 1 might connect the first "bank" to "river" (nature context).
• Head 2 might connect the second "bank" to "building" (finance context).
Both heads run in parallel to figure out the different meanings instantly.

cards.title: Myth: More Heads = Slower Speed
cards.body: People often think multi-head attention makes models slower because they are doing more work. Actually, because these heads run in parallel, they work together at the exact same time, making the model incredibly fast and efficient on modern computer chips!

cards.title: Visualizing Parallel Paths
cards.body: Instead of a single, narrow spotlight trying to scan a whole stage, we use multiple spotlights at the same time to see the whole picture.

left.text: One spotlight scanning the sentence step-by-step. Slow and misses the big picture.

right.text: Multiple spotlights shining in parallel. Fast and captures all angles at once.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of running multiple attention heads in parallel?

options.text: It makes the AI write much longer sentences.
It allows the model to focus on different aspects of context simultaneously.
It stops the AI from repeating words.
It deletes useless words from the input.

exercises.explain: Multi-head attention lets the model look at different relationships (like grammar, meaning, and references) at the same time, rather than just focusing on one thing.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Multi-head attention runs its attention heads at the same time rather than one after the other.
exercises.explain: Multi-head attention is designed specifically to run its heads in parallel, saving time and capturing different context clues simultaneously.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how multi-head attention operates:
exercises.sentence: Multi-head attention runs multiple self-attention heads in _______ to analyze different parts of a sentence at the same time.
exercises.explain: By running in parallel, the attention heads don't have to wait for each other, allowing the AI to process sentences much faster.

exercises.prompt: Three AI models explain how multi-head attention works. Which model makes a mistake?

options.text: AI model A says: "Multi-head attention splits the work so different heads focus on different word relationships at the same time."
AI model B says: "Multi-head attention must run each head one after another, which slows down the process."
AI model C says: "Running attention heads in parallel helps the model capture a richer, multi-angled view of the text."

exercises.explain: The second statement is incorrect because multi-head attention runs its heads in parallel, not one after the other.

exercises.prompt: How does multi-head attention process information to understand context quickly and deeply?

options.text: It runs them one after another, waiting for the first head to finish before starting the next.
It runs multiple attention heads in parallel, looking at different context details at the same time.
It turns off half of the heads to save computer memory and speed up the training.
It forces all heads to focus on the exact same word so the model doesn't get confused.

exercises.explain: Multi-head attention splits the task so that multiple self-attention heads can run at the same time (in parallel), allowing the model to analyze different types of connections simultaneously.
